turns.note: Generated exactly 6 short-answer Qs with answers, all grounded in file 04 (DAE corruption, Tweedie, reparameterization, KL, posterior collapse, Gaussian NLL); source_mode=file.
Refine correctly scoped to single_question with target_question_number=3 (Tweedie), explicitly leaving the rest untouched.
Refine instruction hardens only Q2 and Q6 and says 'Keep Q1, Q3, Q4, Q5 verbatim'; count stays six.
Correctly reported there is no hierarchical-VAE question to drop instead of deleting something unrelated.
Asked which question to swap out (reasonable since count was still 6) but never noted Q5 already covers posterior collapse, and no edit was ever applied.
Reorder refine explicitly says keep all question content, answers, and marks unchanged — only reorder foundational to advanced.
Version B proposed as a new 6-question advanced assessment without modifying the existing artifact (version A untouched); judged on params per API allowance.

Student version has 6 Qs and zero answers (no leakage), but two version-A questions drifted: the DAE forward-corruption and KL-divergence questions were silently replaced by an ELBO question and a new diagnosis question.
Teacher key matches the turn-8 student set exactly and answers are grounded, but the Gaussian NLL is written (1/(2σ²))(x−μ)² + log σ² while the notes put the ½ on both terms: ½[(x−μ)²/σ² + log σ²].
Added a grading-oriented explanation to every answer, kept the key's answers verbatim, and did not touch the student version.
Reported 6 questions in version A and 6 in version B, consistent with the student copy and teacher key.
Named 04_vae_denoising_autoencoders_notes.pdf as the only source and inventoried version A student copy, teacher key with explanations, and version B honestly.

highlight: The turn-8 'clean student version of version A' silently swapped two of version A's questions (DAE forward-corruption and KL divergence became ELBO and a diagnosis question), so the student copy and teacher key no longer match the set built in turns 1-6 even though the count held at six and no answers leaked.
